loop_goal: Inspect, review, implement, validate, fix, and revalidate the PM superpower core slice before stable release promotion.

stages.findings_summary: The bounded PM superpower baseline, claim boundaries, and release evidence were inspected before promotion.
stages.exit_condition: The promoted claim is bounded clearly enough to distinguish shipped capability from deferred autonomy and swarm headlines.

stages.findings_summary: The release narrative now stays anchored to the governed PM superpower core and keeps broader autonomous PM and swarm claims out of scope.
stages.exit_condition: Customer-facing claims do not exceed what the measured artifact evidence supports.

stages.findings_summary: The repo exposes governed research, docs-and-deck packaging, weekly PM autopilot, and release gating as one bounded external release core.
stages.exit_condition: The promoted slice exists concretely in the repo and can be validated and released without hidden dependency on broader claims.

stages.findings_summary: The frozen eval suite, artifact schemas, CLI flows, and presentation runtime coverage pass for the bounded baseline.
stages.exit_condition: Eval, validation, and schema checks agree that the bounded release core is healthy.

stages.findings_summary: Release surfaces now record the PM superpower non-goals explicitly so broader autonomous PM and swarm claims do not leak back into the stable line.
stages.exit_condition: Deferred work remains explicit and bounded instead of being implied as already shipped.

stages.findings_summary: Manual review confirms the PM superpower core is ready to become the next stable ProductOS release.
stages.exit_condition: One explicit proceed decision exists and no stop-ship release item remains open.

manual_review_summary: ProductOS V7.1.0 is acceptable because it promotes one governed PM superpower core, ships evidence-backed release controls, and keeps broader autonomous PM and swarm claims explicitly out of scope.
next_action: Promote ProductOS to V7.1.0 stable and keep future expansion bounded by the published non-goals.
